hero.tagline: Some things wake beneath the earth. A hollow knight. A child in the mist. A forest that remembers.
hero.signupLine: Watch below, like, comment and share on YouTube. It helps more than you know.
hero.footerLine: Not every guardian was meant to protect.

characters.title: Characters
characters.subtitle: In the caverns beneath the world, even broken things remember how to protect.

list.name: Sol
list.tagline: An empty knight with a lantern-shaped head, no body, no voice, and a duty he does not understand.
list.description: Sol is not a legendary warrior. He is damaged, awkward, and strangely innocent, an old armor awakened in the depths of a blue cavern. He cannot truly speak. Only hollow metallic sounds, creaks, groans, and quiet gestures reveal what he feels. Yet when danger comes, he places himself between Rose and the world.

list.name: Rose
list.tagline: A quiet child whose presence makes life return where nothing should grow.
list.description: Rose is small, calm, and almost unreal. She walks through ruined places with a strange serenity, as if she can hear what the world has forgotten. Around her, flowers bloom, roots move, and the dead earth sometimes remembers how to breathe. But Rose is not a weapon, not a miracle to spend, and not a symbol. She is a child.

list.name: The Sleeping Machines
list.tagline: Colossal mechanical-organic beings, half dead, half dreaming beneath moss and roots.
list.description: Long after the old catastrophe, the machines still lie buried in the blue forests. Their bodies are rusted, cracked, and covered in fungi, but some eyes still open in the dark. They do not hate. They do not forgive. They awaken like forgotten disasters.

list.name: The Masked Pilgrims
list.tagline: Figures in black hoods and white masks, carrying strange rituals through the mist.
list.description: Seen only from afar, they move through the cavern platforms in silent processions. Their gongs echo across the abyss. Nobody knows if they are guardians, mourners, or something older. Their presence means the depths are not empty.

knights.title: The Thirteen Knights
knights.subtitle: A procession of hollow armors beneath the earth, from 13 to 1. Two faces are known; the rest will be revealed in coming episodes.
knights.hiddenTeaser: Their identity will be revealed in a coming episode.

12.name: Lanterne
12.title: The Silent Knight
12.tagline: He has no voice. No body. Only a child to protect.
12.description:
Lanterne is the 12th Knight, and one of the weakest among the thirteen.

He awakens alone on an ancient altar, animated by a strange blue energy. There is no body inside his armor. He cannot speak. He cannot write. He cannot explain who he is or what he remembers.

Around his neck, he carries a pendant showing the face of Rose, a mysterious Source Child connected to the human life he lost. When Rose is near, the pendant glows with blue light.

Lanterne is not a traditional hero. He stumbles, hesitates, falls, and observes the world with the fragile attention of someone who has forgotten how to exist.

But beneath his silence, something remains: warmth.

He may be one of the weakest knights, but he may also be the only one capable of protecting Rose without losing what makes him human.

9.name: Serrure
9.title: The Lock Knight
9.tagline: His kindness is real. His oath is dangerous.
9.description:
Serrure is the 9th Knight.

Unlike many of the other oath-bound knights, Serrure has not lost his warmth. He is gentle, welcoming, and strangely reassuring in a world where every knight could become a rival.

When Lanterne is weak, lost, and unable to understand the danger around him, Serrure becomes one of the first signs that not every knight is an enemy.

He speaks with kindness. He helps Lanterne understand the oath, the Source Children, and the path toward the Forgotten Garden.

But Serrure is not free.

Like every knight, he is bound to his own Source Child: Aren, a boy with red and white hair whose awakening closes the first episode.

Serrure's warmth makes him precious.
His oath makes him dangerous.
And one day, his kindness may be tested by the child he was waiting for.

vision.headline: Creating an Anime With AI, One Scene at a Time

hero.title: Lost Garden
hero.subtitle: An independent animated dark fantasy series from a one-person studio.
hero.badge: 17-minute animated episode · 65,000+ views · 9,000+ TikTok likes · Solo AI-assisted production
hero.emotional: A silent knight awakens in a forgotten underground world, guided by the memory of a mysterious child named Rose.

storyAngle.title: Why this story matters to the press
storyAngle.lead: Lost Garden is not a technical demo. It is a complete animated episode, written, directed, and produced independently by Frank Houbre. The project shows how new creative tools can help independent creators build ambitious animated worlds that once required large studio teams and budgets.

angles.title: Independent creation
angles.text: A single creator produces a complete 17-minute animated episode.

angles.title: Innovation
angles.text: An AI-assisted audiovisual production workflow designed as a tool in service of artistic direction.

angles.title: Industry
angles.text: A new way to produce ambitious animated series with small teams, without replacing human vision.

episode.title: Episode 1 — The Awakening of the Lantern Knight
episode.synopsis: In the first episode of Lost Garden, Lanterne, a silent and fragile knight, awakens in a vast underground world of blue forests, ancient ruins, ghostly creatures, and forgotten machines. Guided by a luminous medallion, he sets out to find Rose, a mysterious child who may hold the key to this world's future.

watch.title: Watch the Episode
watch.lead: Journalists, partners, and curators can watch the full episode online before requesting an interview, visuals, or additional information.

audience.title: Audiences loved it
audience.intro: Released in early June on TikTok, the episode drew hundreds of enthusiastic comments within days. A selection of authentic audience reactions below.

comments.author: P_trl.cw
comments.text: No studio and this guy quietly drops an episode this sensational??

comments.author: Ley
comments.text: Honestly, it's impressive. He made a full episode alone with genuinely strong animation quality. Meanwhile major studios take years for a handful of episodes. What he pulled off solo is remarkable.

comments.author: a_mine
comments.text: The animation is better than TBATE's

comments.author: victor_matu pro
comments.text: I think it's really great. The real question is: could we do this ourselves? AI is just a modern tool. Behind every creation there's always someone bringing a vision. The result is genuinely impressive.

comments.author: Nicolas Barsac
comments.text: It's better made than 70% of anime out there

comments.author: Noblem
comments.text: That opening OST gave me chills. Worthy of the greatest soundtracks.

comments.author: Olivier Trijeaud
comments.text: It's simply sublime, I loved it. Bravo and thank you for this delicious moment.

comments.author: The Maddos
comments.text: Doesn't even look like AI. It's strong.

comments.author: Art&Co
comments.text: It's exceptional, it deserves so much more visibility.

comments.author: mob_J19
comments.text: So powerful and beautiful. You're a boss.

comments.author: Swanny Lanoix
comments.text: The OST, the sound design… Several moments are so well done I forgot it was AI. Exactly the kind of work that gives project creators real hope.

comments.author: okami_rya
comments.text: I just watched something this incredible for free? Even with AI involved, it's the first time I've been this absorbed by a world I knew nothing about from episode one.

comments.author: Digital Business Succes
comments.text: Bravo, it's incredible. It really looks like anime from a proper studio. The story is engaging, you want more, and the music lands beautifully.

comments.author: yassersoulaimane3
comments.text: Keep going man, it's just incredible. Don't ever give up!

comments.author: Zirokss
comments.text: This is incredible, seriously well done.

pressRelease.title: Press Release
pressRelease.headline: Independent creator completes 17-minute animated series solo using AI-assisted production workflow
paragraphs: At a time when producing an animated series typically requires dozens, if not hundreds, of people, Frank Houbre took on an unusual challenge: producing a complete episode of an original animated series entirely on his own.
The result is Lost Garden, a dark fantasy series set in a vast underground world populated by knights, mysterious children, and forgotten creatures.

Released in early June, the first episode has already surpassed 65,000 views in just a few days, drawing enthusiastic comments from viewers impressed by the visual quality, art direction, and ambition of the project.
Over several weeks, Frank Houbre handled the entire production solo: writing, art direction, storyboard, animation, editing, sound design, music, and post-production.

Contrary to some assumptions, the project was not created by pressing a single button. The story, characters, world, narrative structure, and artistic choices were conceived and directed by its creator. Artificial intelligence served as a production accelerator, enabling an independent creator to realize a vision that would previously have required a full studio.
Beyond Lost Garden, Frank Houbre is also developing ScreenWeaver, software for screenwriters and filmmakers that bridges scriptwriting and AI-assisted storyboard visualization.
This approach reflects a broader shift in the audiovisual industry, where AI tools are gradually becoming creative assistants in service of writers, directors, and studios.
pressRelease.quote: My goal is not to replace artists or studios. On the contrary. I believe these tools will enable a new generation of creators to tell stories they could never have afforded to produce before.

aboutFrank.title: About Frank Houbre
aboutFrank.bio: Frank Houbre is a French entrepreneur and creator working at the intersection of artificial intelligence, storytelling, marketing, and audiovisual production. Through projects like Lost Garden, ScreenWeaver, and AI Studios, he explores how new creative workflows can help independent creators produce ambitious stories with leaner teams.
aboutFrank.portraitAlt: Portrait of Frank Houbre, creator of Lost Garden

quotes.title: Quotes
quotes.intro: Pull quotes for articles, interviews, and press coverage.
items: Lost Garden shows that a solo creator can today build an animated world that would have been impossible to produce alone just a few years ago.
AI did not replace the creative process. It let me go further, faster, with an ambition I could never have reached on my own.
This is not about removing the human from animation. It is about giving independent creators the means to finally tell the stories they have carried for years.
quotes.attribution: Frank Houbre, creator of Lost Garden
